taskDescription: Click on the key embryology terms related to fetal membranes in the text below.
textField:
The *chorion* is the outermost fetal membrane, formed by the *trophoblast* and extraembryonic *somatic* *mesoderm*. The *chorion* *frondosum* has proliferating *villi* and faces the *decidua* *basalis*, forming the fetal part of the *placenta*. The smooth *chorion* *laeve* faces the *decidua* *capsularis*.

The *amnion* lines the amniotic cavity. After 16 weeks, *amniotic* fluid is mainly fetal *urine*. The fetus *swallows* fluid to regulate volume. Excess fluid (*polyhydramnios*) may indicate *oesophageal* *atresia* or *anencephaly*. Reduced fluid (*oligohydramnios*) may result from renal *agenesis*, causing *Potter* sequence with *pulmonary* *hypoplasia*.

The human *placenta* is *haemochorial* — maternal blood bathes chorionic villi directly. The *umbilical* cord contains two *umbilical* *arteries* and one *umbilical* *vein*, embedded in *Wharton's* jelly.

overallFeedback.feedback: Review fetal membranes and try again.
